messages.content: summarize: Therefore, I began my search for a professor at an accredited German university whose research aligns with my interests in digital health. After extensive searching, I discovered that Dr. Till Winkler is a leading professor in digital health research. Dr. Winkler holds the Chair of Information Management at the University of Hagen. His research on various Information Systems phenomena, particularly in the field of digital health across different countries, has been published in reputable journals such as the Journal of Management Information Systems, Journal of the Association of Information Systems, MIS Quarterly Executive, Electronic Markets, and Health Policy & Technology. Dr. Winkler has been recognized with the Early Career Award from the Association of Information Systems. He also serves in editorial roles, including as an Associate Editor for Business and Information Systems Engineering (BISE). Furthermore, he has a proven track record of successfully acquiring and managing third-party funding for research projects totaling over 2 million Euro. With this in mind, I contacted Dr. Till Winkler to express my interest in a research opportunity in the field of digital health. Dr. Winkler reviewed my curriculum vitae and cover letter and subsequently held an interview with me. After evaluating my qualifications, he concluded that I am well-suited to conduct a research project in the digital health field. Following his guidance, I dedicated 3-4 months to working on a research proposal focused on digital health adoption titled “The Influence of Long-Term Orientation on Patient Attitudes Towards Digital Health: A Cross-Country Study in Germany and Iran.” Four months after submitting the proposal, it was approved by the relevant committee and awarded a fund of 13,104 Euro. As a result, I commenced my funded research project in July 2022. Due to the significance of the project, my research has progressed significantly, and I am currently working on five important research articles in the field. These articles are expected to be completed by the end of August 2024. Recognizing the importance of continuing these research projects and being satisfied with my work, the University of Hagen extended my stay until the end of August 2024.

The author searched for a professor in digital health research at a German university and found Dr. Till Winkler, who is a leading expert in the field. Dr. Winkler has published research on digital health in reputable journals and has received awards for his work. The author contacted Dr. Winkler and after a review of their qualifications, Dr. Winkler agreed to supervise their research project. The author's research proposal was approved and funded, and they began their research project in July 2022. The project has progressed well, and the author is currently working on five research articles in the field. The University of Hagen has recognized the importance of the research and extended the author's stay until the end of August 2024.

edit: Therefore, I began my search for a professor at an accredited German university whose research aligns with my interests in digital health. After extensive searching, I discovered that Dr. Till Winkler is a leading professor in digital health research. Dr. Winkler holds the Chair of Information Management at the University of Hagen. His research has been published in reputable journals such as the Journal of Management Information Systems, Journal of the Association of Information Systems, MIS Quarterly Executive, and Health Policy & Technology. Dr. Winkler has been recognized with the Early Career Award from the Association of Information Systems. He also serves in editorial roles, including as an Associate Editor for Business and Information Systems Engineering (BISE). Furthermore, he has a proven track record of successfully acquiring and managing third-party funding for research projects. With this in mind, I contacted Dr. Till Winkler to express my interest in a research opportunity in the field of digital health. Dr. Winkler evaluated my qualifications and determined that I am well-suited to conduct a research project in digital health after conducting an interview and carefully considering my expertise and skills. So, I prepared a research proposal focused on digital health adoption titled “The Influence of Long-Term Orientation on Patient Attitudes Towards Digital Health: A Cross-Country Study in Germany and Iran.” Fortunately, it was approved and I awarded a fund of 13,104 Euro. As a result, I commenced my funded research project at the university of Hagen in July 2022. Due to the significance of the project, my research has progressed significantly, and I am currently working on five important research articles in the field. These articles are expected to be completed by the end of August 2024. Recognizing the importance of continuing these research projects and being satisfied with my work, the University of Hagen extended my stay until the end of August 2024.

SUMRAIZE: Particularly noteworthy is the adoption of the Digital Healthcare Act by the German parliament on November 7, 2019. This act, which has gained approval from the Federal Council and the German President, positions Germany as a leader in digital health policy.

The Digital Healthcare Act, approved by the German parliament on November 7, 2019, marks a significant milestone in digital health policy. It has received approval from the Federal Council as well as the German President, establishing Germany as a frontrunner in the field of digital health policy.

Dr. Winkler's impressive credentials, publication record, and funding success made him an ideal mentor for the author's research project. edit: This groundbreaking legislation has propelled Germany to the forefront of digital health policy, not only by encouraging the use of digital health solutions but also by facilitating the utilization of health data for research purposes.

Germany's groundbreaking Digital Healthcare Act has positioned the country as a leader in digital health policy. This legislation promotes the adoption of digital health solutions and supports the use of health data for research, further advancing Germany's position in the field.
